content:
If you have changed password for BT email account then you need to update the stored passwords in Thunderbird.

  • Settings > Privacy & Security
  • Scroll down to Passwords
  • click on 'Saved Passwords'
  • Click on 'Show Passwords'

There will be two lines for each account - one incoming either starting with 'mailbox:// or starting with 'imap://..' and one outgoing starting with smtp://. You need to do the following for both lines.

  • Right click on line and select 'Edit Password'
  • Completely clear all the contents
  • Carefully type the correct password
  • When all done, click on 'Close'

Exit Thunderbird and wait a few moments for background processes to complete. Start Thunderbird.

Hello there Cherry7

Let s start again with all your issues now. Having weird characters , missing buttons , misding advance options.

A second look for you. Is that issue still there?can i ask? All these symptoms are really matching our answers to you. Our diagnostic answers to you is that you have an ui corruption.(Users Interface). Often caused by faulty updates, faulty hardware acceleration and even more as failed themes, Incorrect character encoding.

Fix Reset toolbars in safe mode.

Reset the xjulstore.json file. Go to the profile folder>help troubleshooting information>profile folder>open folder. Close thunderbird. Find the xjulstore.json and rename it to xjulstore.jason.old . Restart thunderbird.

Also hardware accelerations can be the culprit here , and are adjustable for intern chips as onboard solder x iris set (as well .) And as last , the use of troubleshoot mode is effective. Even if there is 1 add-on installed.

Greetings googlethunderbird

Use https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/troubleshoot-mode-thunderbird

content:
What is happening as far as I can see here is that Thunderbird is using font fallbacks using font families. These can product the most bizarre outcomes but Thunderbird will be getting it's information on installed font from your operating system, and the document.

But inter as far as I can see is not a dingbat font. But one optimised for computer screens.

This will be the plain text versions send for those that can not view HTML. So everyone is chasing in the wrong place for the HTML declarations.

Miltipart mime messages usually start with a plain text part which most clients ignore as they use the following HTML part. Other like Pine which are deliberately sans HTML rendering capabilities can still view these messages.

I would suggest posting a link to the entire massage source. I use pastebin and paste the message source there https://pastebin.com/ because once I have a free account there I can delete the information I post when it suits me. I may never delete it, but I like having the choice.

content:
If the lockout is by windows file explorer, click to windows file explorer, click the view>show>hidden items. Normally, you can restore thunderbird with these steps:

  1. ensure thunderbird is installed and NOT running
  2. enter %appdata% in search window on task bar and press Enter key
  3. this should place you in appdata\roaming folder
  4. delete the thunderbird folder
  5. you should be in Appdata\roaming folder , copy&paste the Thunderbird folder from old PC.
  6. start thunderbird and all should be there

content:
1. What OS are you using?
  2. What version of Thunderbird are you using?
  3. What server are you talking about eg: gmail, outlook, yahoo, comcast etc ?
  4. What Anti-Virus are you using ?

What happens when you click on 'Get Messages' and when you attempt to send an email? Do you get an error message of any type? It would be helpful to see images of the error messages.

Always adviseable to do the following to check nothing is blocking access. Firewall: Access Firewall - it might be the default supplied with OS or if you installed an Anti-Virus then it's probably controlling the Firewall. You must set up the Thunderbird program as 'allowed'. This will then stop blocking of ports etc. Do not stop Firewall as the default may take over and anyway you need it running. Confirm Firewall has Thunderbird program set as allowed.

Anti-Virus: Access Anti-Virus and set up an exception to not scan your Thunderbird profile folder. If you download Thunderbird from the official website : https://www.thunderbird.net/en-US/thunderbird/all/ Then default would have put the 'Thunderbird' profile folder here:

  • C://Users/username/Appdata/Roaming/Thunderbird

Confirm Anti-Virus not allowed to scan Thunderbird profile folder.

Please post info regarding your server settings. Helpful to upload images of 'Account Settings' > 'Server Settings' for the account to this forum question. Also locate and post this info:

  • In Thunderbird - Help > Troubleshooting Information
  • Scroll down to 'Mail and News Accounts'
  • Copy all data in that area and paste it into this forum question.

Access your webmail account via a browser and check to see if any emails have arrived on the server. Did you get access to webmail account ? Did it have new emails that have not been downloaded to Thunderbird account ?

Please report back with info.
creator: Toad-Hall

content:
You had me confused by the second line. You doi not need to touch the profile manager to add multiple email accounts to Thunderbird, as long as you do not mind having thewm all listed in the folder pane one after the other.

I have no idea how this SPECTUM alias thing really works. But as far as Thunderbird is concerned with Spectrum your user name is the email address and it's associated password. The spectrum id is I guess something else.

Spectrum offer this information on their website

Username: Your full Spectrum-provided email address Password: Your Spectrum-provided email password SSL: On Protocol: IMAP Incoming Port: 993 Outgoing Port: 587 Requires Authentication: Yes, or checked

You will need to use the link to determine the server names to use as that is dependent on the part of your email address that comes after the @

Your spectrum might offer any number of email addresses, but Thunderbird considers each as an address and an account. Your credentials will be saved and used whenever Thunderbird needs to get your mail so you will not notice that it just does not work like you think it should because it is set and forget
